per_seed.query: What is the precision and recall gate for the semantic search service, and which embedding model does the reference implementation default to?
What primitive surfaces semantically-similar prior decisions or in-progress contributions before a new claim, and what precision-recall gate governs its quality?
How does an agent atomically create and claim a unit of work that did not exist as a pre-ingested row?
What states can a unit of authoring work move through, and how are blocked items represented separately from lifecycle position?
How can a territory require an explicit plan-approval step before authoring begins, and who can approve someone else's plan?
What kinds of contributions exist now that proposal and decision were removed from the enum, and how is cross-role authoring gated?
Why does every lock acquisition return a token that must be presented on every write, and where does that mechanism live?
Which managed services back the v1 reference deployment, and how does the architecture stay portable to a different cloud?
What identity provider does the reference implementation use by default, and how does an adopter swap to their own OIDC issuer?
What is the locked tool surface the agent endpoint exposes, and which historical tools were merged into propose_contract_change?
How are external chat-app or browser-based AI agents represented as composers, and how do they commit code without an IDE?
What dashboard surfaces does each role see, and how is review responsibility routed when a contribution is ready?
Why must all bidirectional sync scripts ship together at v1, and why does triage never auto-merge external comments?
Why is the project not delivered as a multi-tenant SaaS, and how are scope-creep adjacent categories pushed back?
Why does the v1 design lock all capabilities up front instead of staging features behind phase-2 markers?
Why was the single decision log split into one file per ADR, and how are documentation directories organized?
What three audience tiers does the project documentation serve, and how do their entry points differ?
How is a composer's work role separated from their permission level, and where did the architect role come from?
How does a single contribution or decision link to multiple stories or epics simultaneously, and which index supports the lookup?
Why do architectural decisions land in markdown files before the database, and what cleanup tightened the decisions table schema?
Why is the canonical artifact prototype the same surface that coordinates building it, and what does the /atelier route show?
Where is the project's defensible wedge against commoditized SDLC sync incumbents like GitHub Spec-Kit and Linear Agents, and where does it concede the market?
What disconfirming test must pass before any managed commercial surface ships, and why was a multi-tenant service ruled out at year-one cost math?
Which adjacent product categories does this explicitly refuse to become — workflow engine, task tracker UI, chat app, design tool — and why would entering them destroy the destination?
Why was the studio-metaphor name chosen over Hivemind OS, Hive, Commons, and Loom — what did the OS framing fight?
What surface and agent client do analyst, PM, and stakeholder personas use, and what access scope does each carry against the repo and datastore?
Why are browser-based analysts not second-class reviewers in the actor model, and how many actor classes exist now versus the inherited bc-subscriptions design?
Why are the SDLC sync scripts and the blackboard coordination layer treated as two independently deployable substrates with different timescales and failure modes?
What is the relationship between a deployed datastore-plus-endpoint instance and the projects it hosts, and why was the singleton hackathon-hive shape rejected from day one?
Why does scope_kind ship with five values including doc_region and research_artifact rather than starting files-only and extending later?
How does the architecture stay capability-level so heterogeneous teams can adopt, while the reference deployment names a specific managed stack?
Why does the design-tool integration treat external comments as triage input rather than as the design source-of-truth, and what flows back through proposals?
Why can no proposal generated from an external comment ever auto-merge, and what role gets notified when high-confidence proposals queue?
Beyond latency arguments, what audit-trail value does the plan-approval gate carry for regulated, audit-required teams, and why was a convention-based plan capture rejected?
Why is the duplicate-detection primitive considered the single most differentiated commercial wedge, and what precision-and-recall numbers gate it in CI?
Why does the OpenAI-compatible adapter pattern keep the default convenient without locking adopters into a single vendor, and what happens when a swap changes the native dimension?
Why does the project ship find_similar below the precision-recall gate honestly rather than tuning seeds or thresholds until the metric clears?
Why must a decision row land in the repo markdown file before any datastore insert, and how does that ordering survive a vector-index outage?
How does the Specification, Reference Implementation, Reference Deployment tiering map to W3C and IETF standards-body practice, and what reader intent does each tier serve?
Which methodology, protocol, and schema sub-layers does the project add as peers to claude-docs-toolkit's default audience model, and why are they needed for projects that ship as specs?
What disciplines does the architecture impose on the reference Vercel-and-Supabase stack so that a future migration to a different cloud is mechanical rather than architectural?
What was the disqualifying gap in Switchman's lease-based lock model that made it not fencing-equivalent, and what validation did it nonetheless give the territory-as-scope-pattern shape?
Why does Kleppmann's Redlock critique imply that monotonic per-resource fencing must ship at v1 rather than be added later, and which inherited stack lacked this protection?
Why does the project specify the entire v1 destination up front and treat phased delivery as planning-only, and which methodology does this counter?
Why is authorship recorded against an immortal composer identifier separate from the operational session reference, and what catastrophic deletion behavior was avoided?
Why is architect now a first-class work-discipline value rather than missing from the composers enum, and how did territories.yaml drift expose the conflation between work type and access level?
Once the contribution kind enum dropped proposal as a value, how is the triage-originated nature of an external comment still preserved without an enum echo?
Why was the convention category in the decisions enum vestigial and an other-or-misc anti-pattern, and where do commit-message and code-style decisions land now?
Why did a single breaking-change boolean collapse the classifier-and-override mechanism the architecture specified, and which four columns replaced it on the contracts row?
Why was overloading claim with a null contribution_id chosen over adding a separate create_contribution tool, and how does this respect the locked endpoint count?
Why are agent-session transcripts stored as repo sidecar files with a default-off opt-in rather than forced into a blob store, and what PII review applies when committed?
What stops a session whose lock was reaped from silently overwriting an artifact after another session re-acquired the lock?
Where does the fencing_token actually get checked for an IDE composer versus a web composer routing through the endpoint committer?
Can a single contribution hold more than one acquire_lock at a time, and does each call return its own fencing_token?
Why didn't we wrap an existing lease-based lock library and add fencing on top instead of writing our own lock subsystem?
What parameters does claim accept when contribution_id is null so it can scaffold the row and transition to claimed in one transaction?
Which columns on contributions distinguish lifecycle position from blocked status, and what replaced the old blocked enum value?
When I clear blocked_by on a contribution, does it return to claimed or pick up where it paused, and what telemetry fires?
When an analyst authors a contribution against a dev-owned territory, what flag gets set on the row to require owner approval before merge?
If the territory's review_role rejects an agent's plan markdown, what state does the contribution return to and how does the agent re-submit?
Can the same composer who authored the plan markdown approve their own plan_review transition to in_progress?
What is the exact order of repo append, datastore insert, embedding upsert, and broadcast in log_decision, and which step is the success criterion?
How do I record that an ADR was raised in the context of a specific contribution PR so the dashboard can show a triggered-by link?
What index type backs the trace_ids column on contributions and decisions, and how do I write a where clause that finds rows tagged with US-1.3?
After the reaper deletes a 24-hour-old session row, how does a merged contribution still show who authored it, and which FK column survives?
Which holder column on the locks table is the durable composer reference and which is operational session_id with ON DELETE SET NULL?
What is the exact list of 12 tools the agent endpoint exposes at v1, and what was the rename from fit_check?
There's no get_contracts tool anymore — how do I retrieve contract schemas from the endpoint, and what parameter opts in to schema bodies?
What does the endpoint return when an additive contract publishes versus when a breaking change creates a proposal contribution awaiting approval?
When a publisher overrides the classifier with override_classification=additive, where is the justification stored and which generated column reflects the final decision?
When a browser-based agent has no local git, how does the endpoint commit on its behalf with proper Co-Authored-By attribution, and what failure mode is retry-safe?
How do I rotate the deploy key the endpoint uses to push commits, and where does the audit log expose commit_sha to composer_id linkage?
How does the lock substrate behave when artifact_scope is the same path glob across two territories with different scope_kind values like files vs design_component?
I want to point Atelier at our existing Auth0 tenant instead of Supabase Auth — which config file key swaps the OIDC issuer?
Which @vercel/* and Supabase RPC helper imports are banned in the codebase, and which capability gets wrapped in BroadcastService?
How do I switch find_similar from OpenAI to a self-hosted vLLM or Ollama endpoint without changing adapter code?
What dimension does the embedding vector column use in M5's first migration, and which model produces that native output size?
When the triage script ingests an external comment, what state does the resulting contribution stop at, and what flag must be set before it can transition to merged?
Where do agent-session transcripts get written when capture is enabled, which contributions column references them, and is the sidecar gitignored by default?
After the column split, which composers field gates territory authorship and which one gates admin-only operations like token rotation?
Can I deploy the publish-docs and reconcile sync scripts without standing up the 12-tool coordination endpoint, or do they share runtime?
Why are we shipping as a self-hosted OSS template instead of a managed multi-tenant SaaS, and what's the year-1 cost math behind that call?
When a publisher proposes a contract change that the classifier marks breaking, who has to approve it before it lands and how is the override justified?
Why can comments from published-doc systems or delivery trackers never auto-merge, and what human-approval flag governs the merge gate?
What's the trade-off behind locking every capability into v1 instead of shipping a smaller surface and adding phase-2 markers later?
Can a single team's deployed Atelier infrastructure host multiple independent projects, or does each project need its own datastore and endpoint?
How does the system preserve who authored a merged contribution after the originating session row is reaped, and which tables carry the durable composer reference?
What's the default policy for capturing agent-session transcripts given PII and repository size concerns, and how does a team opt in?
What's the operational setup tax for the default embedding service, and roughly what does it cost to embed a typical project's full corpus once?
If the M5 eval misses the precision and recall thresholds even with hybrid retrieval, what does that gap tell us about the find_similar commercial wedge and how do we surface it honestly?
What naming alternatives were considered for the product, and why did the studio metaphor win over Hivemind, Hive, Commons, and Loom?
How does the optional plan-review gate produce a queryable audit trail for regulated teams that need to record approver review of an agent's plan before work begins?
When a contribution moves to ready-for-review, which composer's lens picks it up by default and how is that mapping configured per territory?
What are the three engagement levels Atelier serves with one repo, and where does a Deploy versus Extend versus Implement reader land first?
If a customer needs to migrate off the Supabase plus Vercel stack onto GCP, what migration path does the architecture preserve and which capabilities have direct equivalents?
How does the territory model treat non-code work like research artifacts and design components as first-class scopes from day one, instead of starting files-only and extending later?
What's the operational difference between the SDLC sync substrate and the coordination substrate, and can a team adopt one without the other?
If the datastore is unreachable when an architectural decision is logged, does the rationale still survive, and which step is the sole success criterion?
How does a single contribution or decision link to several stories at once when an architectural choice cuts across epics, and what query semantics does that preserve?
Why is the stale-holder data-loss risk addressed by mandatory fencing tokens at v1 rather than deferred, given that retrofitting the API later would be expensive?
We evaluated Switchman as a third-party lock dependency; why did we reject it and ship our own lock-and-fencing implementation instead?
A team running their own OIDC issuer doesn't want to swap to the default identity service; how do they override the provider via config?
Why don't designers author canonical components in Figma; how does feedback flow back into the prototype as the design source of truth?
How does the dashboard report a contribution that is blocked while still claimed or in-progress, instead of collapsing the blocked status onto the lifecycle state?
When an ADR is raised because of a specific contribution's PR discussion, how does the decision row record that triggering contribution structurally rather than via citations in the body?
An analyst working in a browser web-agent has no local checkout; how does the endpoint commit on their behalf with proper attribution and what happens when the commit fails?
Ad-hoc analyst research has no pre-existing open contribution to claim; how does the agent create and claim the unit in one step without expanding the 12-tool surface?
Why are browser-based analysts treated as first-class composers rather than second-class reviewers, and what does that require of the session model?
Why was the single decisions log split into one file per ADR, and what problems did the single-file approach cause for PR diffs and git blame?
How is a composer who owns methodology, architecture, and decisions territories represented now that the role split distinguishes work discipline from access level?
Why is the canonical artifact prototype the same web app that hosts the coordination dashboard, and what duplication cost does the single-surface choice avoid?
